description: 冷战前欧洲(1946), 1946Q1 to 1958Q2, 25 countries, 50 rounds. Each round=3 months. Following is issue-specific leadership preference (NOT alliance).
following_definition: 追随!=同盟: 追随是对某国在特定议题上的领导偏好, 不等同于战略同盟关系。盟友可能在经济议题上追随敌对阵营的领导者, 这是正常的外交现象。每轮的追随基于该轮最突出议题上的政策协调对象。

key_issue_specific_cases.Yugoslavia(12): Communist state, initially follows USSR(1), breaks with Stalin in 1948 (R10) and becomes independent throughout remaining rounds.
key_issue_specific_cases.Finland(22): Forced to follow USSR(1) on security issues (Finlandization), but maintains Western economic/cultural ties and democratic system.
key_issue_specific_cases.Spain(6): Franco regime remains isolated, not following any major power bloc. Independent throughout.
key_issue_specific_cases.Ireland(23): Neutral throughout, independent of all great powers. UN member but non-aligned.
key_issue_specific_cases.France(3): Initially follows UK(2) in Western camp, but under De Gaulle (R49) becomes independent - key following != alliance case.
key_issue_specific_cases.Sweden(11): Formally neutral, but economically and culturally follows UK/Western camp. Not a NATO member but clearly Western-aligned.

1.dominant_issue: Postwar reconstruction begins - UN established, Iron Curtain not yet descended, wartime cooperation fading

2.dominant_issue: Greek Civil War intensifies & Iran crisis - USSR pressures Turkey/Iran, West resists, Cold War tensions rise

3.dominant_issue: Paris Peace Conference - postwar treaties with Italy/Romania/Hungary/Bulgaria/Finland finalized

4.dominant_issue: Bizone established (US/UK merge German occupation zones) - Western integration begins, USSR objects

5.dominant_issue: Truman Doctrine announced - US pledges to support Greece/Turkey against communism, containment policy begins

6.dominant_issue: Marshall Plan announced - massive US economic aid for Europe, USSR rejects and forbids Eastern Bloc participation

7.dominant_issue: Cominform founded & Marshall Plan implemented - USSR tightens control over Eastern Bloc, Western Europe begins recovery

8.dominant_issue: Communist consolidation in Eastern Europe - Poland/Romania/Bulgaria become people's republics

9.dominant_issue: Czechoslovak communist coup - last democratic Eastern European state falls to communism, West alarmed

10.dominant_issue: Tito-Stalin split - Yugoslavia expelled from Cominform, asserts independent communist path, key following!=alliance case

11.dominant_issue: Berlin Blockade begins - USSR blocks West Berlin, Western Allies organize massive airlift

12.dominant_issue: Berlin Airlift succeeds - Western resolve demonstrated, USSR unable to force West out of Berlin

14.dominant_issue: Berlin Blockade ends & two German states founded - FRG and GDR established, Cold War division institutionalized

15.dominant_issue: Soviet atomic bomb test - USSR acquires nuclear weapons, Cold War enters nuclear age, arms race begins

16.dominant_issue: COMECON formalized - Soviet economic bloc consolidates, Eastern Europe integrated into Soviet economy

17.dominant_issue: Schuman Declaration - European Coal and Steel Community proposed, Franco-German reconciliation begins

18.dominant_issue: Korean War begins - North Korea invades South, Cold War goes hot in Asia, NATO rearmed, Western solidarity peaks

19.dominant_issue: Korean War - UN counteroffensive, Inchon landing, NATO establishes integrated command (SHAPE)

20.dominant_issue: China enters Korean War - massive Chinese offensive, UN forces retreat, fear of wider war

21.dominant_issue: Korean War stalemate & European Defense Community proposed - front stabilizes, West German rearmament debated

22.dominant_issue: ECSC Treaty signed - European integration advances, Franco-German economic cooperation institutionalized

23.dominant_issue: Korean War armistice talks begin - military stalemate, negotiations start at Kaesong/Panmunjom

24.dominant_issue: UK tests atomic bomb - Britain becomes third nuclear power, NATO nuclear sharing debates begin

25.dominant_issue: NATO Lisbon Conference - ambitious conventional force goals, Greece/Turkey join NATO

26.dominant_issue: European Defense Community treaty signed - attempt to create European army, West Germany to contribute

27.dominant_issue: Stalin's final years - Cold War at peak tension, Soviet domestic repression intensifies, anti-Semitic campaigns

28.dominant_issue: First hydrogen bomb tests - US tests H-bomb (Ivy Mike), USSR follows, thermonuclear age begins

29.dominant_issue: Stalin dies - Malenkov becomes Soviet Premier, succession struggle begins, Cold War thaw signals

30.dominant_issue: East German uprising - workers revolt in GDR, suppressed by Soviet tanks, demonstrates Soviet control fragility

31.dominant_issue: Korean War armistice signed - war ends in stalemate, Cold War military confrontation in Asia de-escalates

32.dominant_issue: USSR tests hydrogen bomb & Khrushchev rises - Soviet nuclear parity approaches, leadership transition continues

33.dominant_issue: Berlin Conference (Big Four) - attempt at German reunification fails, Cold War division persists

34.dominant_issue: Geneva Conference on Indochina - French defeat at Dien Bien Phu, Vietnam partitioned, Geneva Accords

35.dominant_issue: French National Assembly rejects EDC - European army plan collapses, alternative: West German NATO membership

36.dominant_issue: Paris Agreements - West Germany joins NATO, occupation ends, Western European Union formed

37.dominant_issue: Khrushchev consolidates power - Malenkov resigns, Khrushchev becomes dominant, peaceful coexistence policy

38.dominant_issue: Warsaw Pact founded - Soviet response to West German NATO membership, Eastern Bloc military alliance formalized

39.dominant_issue: Austrian State Treaty & Geneva Summit - Austria neutralized, Big Four summit, Spirit of Geneva detente

40.dominant_issue: West Germany joins NATO & USSR- Yugoslavia rapprochement - Khrushchev visits Belgrade, acknowledges Tito's path

41.dominant_issue: Khrushchev's Secret Speech - denounces Stalin's crimes at 20th Party Congress, de-Stalinization begins, Eastern Bloc shaken

42.dominant_issue: Polish October - Gomulka comes to power, Poland asserts limited autonomy while remaining in Warsaw Pact

43.dominant_issue: Hungarian Revolution - Hungary attempts to leave Warsaw Pact, crushed by Soviet invasion, Western condemnation but no intervention

44.dominant_issue: Suez Crisis - UK/France/Israel attack Egypt, US/USSR force withdrawal, demonstrates UK/France no longer independent great powers

45.dominant_issue: Treaty of Rome - EEC and EURATOM founded, European economic integration accelerates, Six sign (France/Italy/Benelux/West Germany)

46.dominant_issue: Sputnik launched - USSR launches first satellite, space race begins, Western technological confidence shaken

47.dominant_issue: NATO dual-track on nuclear weapons - US deploys intermediate-range missiles in Europe, arms control debates

48.dominant_issue: Khrushchev consolidates - Zhukov dismissed, Khrushchev becomes Premier as well as Party Secretary, Soviet leadership unified

49.dominant_issue: De Gaulle returns to power - French Fifth Republic established, France asserts independent foreign policy, challenges UK/US leadership

50.dominant_issue: Berlin Ultimatum begins - Khrushchev demands Western withdrawal from Berlin, second Berlin crisis, Cold War tension peaks
